correct_answer: Fashion has been influenced by culture, technology, politics, and economics.
Clothing in ancient civilizations symbolized status and identity.
Wealthy individuals in ancient times wore elaborate garments made from luxurious fabrics.
Common people in ancient times wore simpler clothing made from materials like linen and wool.
Fashion trends in the Middle Ages were influenced by religion and social hierarchy.
Nobles and royalty in the Middle Ages wore garments made from expensive fabrics like silk and velvet.
Peasants in the Middle Ages wore humble clothing made from rougher materials.
The Renaissance period saw a resurgence of interest in art, culture, and fashion.

correct_answer: The Industrial Revolution brought about mass production of ready-to-wear clothing.
Department stores and fashion magazines democratized fashion in the 18th and 19th centuries.

correct_answer: The Great Depression of the 1930s led to a more conservative approach to fashion.
The post-World War II era saw a resurgence of glamour and luxury in fashion.
The 1950s saw the rise of iconic fashion figures like Audrey Hepburn and Marilyn Monroe.
